# function: jwk_decode

Converts a [JWK](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7517) object back to a PEM key. The round-trip pair for [`jwk_encode`](#function-jwk_encode).

A public JWK becomes a PKIX `PUBLIC KEY`; a private JWK becomes a PKCS#8 `PRIVATE KEY`. EC, RSA, and Ed25519 (OKP) keys are supported.

`options` is reserved for future use; no options are currently defined.

-> Feeding `jwk_encode` output straight back into `jwk_decode` returns a key equivalent to the one you started with.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Convert a JWK object back to PEM. Round-trips with jwk_encode: encoding a key and decoding it returns an equivalent key.
locals {
  ec_key = provider::burnham::ecdsa_p256_key_from_seed("jwk-decode-seed")
  jwk    = provider::burnham::jwk_encode(local.ec_key)
}

output "pem" {
  value = provider::burnham::jwk_decode(local.jwk)
  // → "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n" (equivalent to local.ec_key)
}

// A public JWK decodes to a PUBLIC KEY block.
output "public_pem" {
  value = provider::burnham::jwk_decode(
    provider::burnham::jwk_encode(provider::burnham::x509_self_sign(
      local.ec_key, "example", "0102030405060708", "2001-01-01T00:00:00Z", "2099-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    ))
  )
  // → "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----\n"
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
jwk_decode(jwk dynamic, options dynamic...) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `jwk` (Dynamic) A JWK object (as produced by `jwk_encode`).
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `options` (Variadic, Dynamic) Reserved for future use; no options are currently defined. Pass at most one.
